# Transcrição: 3. Always Clearly Communicate Your Proposal

## [00:00 - 02:49]

Okay, number three rule, always clearly communicate your proposal. Look it's important that you rehearse because if you rehearse you're going to know your presentation and you want to clearly communicate what your offer is. You don't want to mumble, you don't want to cover your mouth, you don't want to barely speak, I think we can do this for you. No man, you want to speak it out there. You want to speak clearly and confidently so that your offer is communicated in a manner that suggests optimism, pride and credibility. Like if you were trying to get everybody out of the theater, hey man come on let's go. See somebody's going to lead the group out of the theater because the place is burning down. That means you've got to really put your voice out there. You can't mumble, you can't whisper, you've got to. So like when I speak to people, when I speak to an audience of a thousand people, I'm not speaking to the first row, I'm speaking to the back row. I want everybody to get the communication. But no one's going to trust you if you don't communicate clearly and confidently and project your voice. Have you ever known somebody that whispers and they're always talking this really low tone and you're always having to say, what would you say? Would you say? It's a little freaking game they play, okay? They're trying to pull you into them. They don't know they're playing this game but you're always saying, what would you say? Would you say, do you trust that communication? Probably not. Do you value it? Would you give it credibility? Probably not. Look, you need to practice this delivery. Do not just assume because you know how to talk that you're communicating clearly. They're completely different things. Now what I did to learn this skill is practiced, I practiced with recorders and video. I'm literally for years. And then I would play those recordings back to ensure that I was coming across, that my communication was actually penetrating, not just hitting. That my communication didn't just get across, it actually made a difference. It didn't just hit them and bounce off, it went through. You get it? I want to penetrate. I want to push the communication through. I want a good, clear voice. I know we can do this, right? Not, I think we can do this. I know we can do this. Here's your proposal I worked out for you. I need your initials here. John, I need you're okay here, here, here and here. Let's get this done. Hey John, follow me, okay? Hey, you want to do this this afternoon or you want to do it tonight? I'm communicating clearly. There'd be any reason you couldn't make it. Look the communication, that penetrating communication where you're actually pushing through is as important as the words you're going to say. Know what you're going to say. Say it with confidence. Think in terms of I'm going to talk in caps. You know capital letters? You ever text somebody in caps and they're like, you screaming at me? We don't want to scream at people but we do want to communicate and we want to communicate clearly. So we're going to take you practice, drill, rehearse, not just you testing. So communicate how clearly.
